CITY HOSPITAL.

BELMONT HOSPITAL. Belmont Street, near Adams Street. 43 Superintendent and Resident Physician-May S. Holmes. Pathologist-Frederick H. Baker. Visiting Physician in Putnam Wards-Albert C. Getchell. Isolation wards for diphtheria and scarlet fever were opened in November, 1896, and have accommodations for the treatment of twenty-five patients. Putnam wards were opened in December, 1914, for the treatment of advanced tuberculosis, and accommodate fifty-five patients. The Hospital is under control of the Board of Health. Patients may be admitted on application to the Board of Health or to Dr. Holmes, Superintendent.

WORCESTER STATE HOSPITAL. Belmont Street, opp. Shrewsbury Street. Branch, Summer Street, between East Central and Asylum. Trustees-Caroline M. Caswell, William J. Delehanty, Edward F. Fletcher, Howard W. Cowee, John H. Perman, Mrs. Anna C. Tatman. Treasurer and Clerk-Jessie M. D. Hamilton. Superintendent-William A. Bryan. Assistant Superintendent-Lewis B. Hill. Steward-Herbert W. Smith. Farmer-William H. Clark. Engineer-James Dickinson, jr. The Worcester State Hospital, a State institution, was opened January 18, 1833. At present there are 2,124 inmates.

GRAFTON STATE HOSPITAL, North Grafton, Mass. Trustees-Frank B. Hall, Chairman; Margaret A. Cashman, Sec.; Francis Prescott, Ernest L. Anderson, Flora M. Cangiano, Enos H. Bigelow, Winslow P. Burhoe. Superintendent-Harlan L. Paine. Asst. Supt.-Hiram L. Horsman; Assistant Physicians-James L. McAuslan, Mary Johnson, Wilbur H. Smith, Frederick H. Salls, Alexander A. Levi, George O. Tessier. The Grafton State Hospital, formerly known as the Worcester State Asylum, was established in accordance with an Act of the Legislature in 1877, and was opened October 23, 1877. The present number of patients is 1,516.

Worcester Country Club-Organized 1913. Clubhouse, Mountain street East. Pres., Charles A. Harrington; Vice-Prests., Harold B. Chase, Merle Bell; Treas., Warren S. Shepard; Sec., Chas. W. Delano. Annual meeting in November.
Worcester County Bar Association-Pres., Arthur J. Young; Vice-Pres., Frank L. Riley; Sec. and Treas., George A. White. Annual meeting in December.

Worcester County Bookkeepers' Association- Org. 1900. Pres., H. G. Walton; Vice-Pres., O. F. Fuller, Blackstone; Sec. and Treas., Josephine Morse, S. Lancaster. organization_name: Worcester County Fish and Game Association
line: Worcester County Fish and Game Association- Org. 1913. Pres., Everett St. John; Sec.-Treas., William L. Allen, 1 Shawmut street.

A library of 3774 volumes is open to the members daily. Election of officers first Wednesday in December.

Worcester County Law Library Association- Org. 1842. Composed of the members of the bar of the county. Library at the Court House. Librarian, George E. Wire; Treas., Thomas H. Gage. The library contains 42,943 volumes. Open daily from 9 to 5 except Saturday afternoons. The books may be consulted by the citizens of the county as well as by the court and bar. Officers elected during March sitting of the Superior Court.

organization_name: Worcester County League of Unitarian Women

Library open every day, Sundays and holidays excepted, from 11 A.M. to 8 P.M.; Saturdays until 9 P.M. Volumes in library over 16,000. Reading room open every day and evening, Sundays and holidays excepted. Annual meeting and election of officers, third Tuesday in April.

Worcester Natural History Society-Org. 1829. 12 State street corner Harvard. This society was united with the Young Men's Library Association in 1856, under the name of the Lyceum and Library Association. In 1866 it was incorporated under the name of the Worcester Lyceum and Natural History Society. In 1884, by an act of the Legislature, the name was changed to that of the Worcester Natural History Society. Directors, Pres., Lemuel F. Woodward; Sec., Joseph H. Perry; Treas., Wendell P. Parker, Charles M. Thayer, Samuel B. Woodward, Mrs. Frank E. Lowe; Acting Custodian, Mrs. John W. Kemp. The museum of the society at 12 State street, is open free to all daily, except Sunday, from 9 a.m. to 12 m. and 2 to 5 p.m. Number of active voting members of the society is limited to fifty.

There are nine Trustees of the City Hospital chosen by the City Council on concurrence, in January, as follows: One from the Board of Aldermen, and two from the Common Council, for one year, and six from the citizens at large for six years.
Commissioners of the Jaques Fund and other funds of the City Hospital-Frank A. Drury, term expires January, 1931; Francis H. Dewey, term expires 1929; John E. White, term expires 1930.

BELMONT HOSPITAL. Belmont Street, near Adams Street. Superintendent and Resident Physician-May S. Holmes. Pathologist-Frederick H. Baker. Visiting Physician in Putnam Wards-Albert C. Getchell. Isolation wards for diphtheria and scarlet fever were opened in November, 1896, and have accommodations for the treatment of 125 patients. Putnam wards were opened in December, 1914, for the treatment of advanced tuberculosis, and accommodate fifty-five patients. The Hospital is under control of the Board of Health. Patients may be admitted on application to the Board of Health or to Dr. Holmes, Superintendent.

WORCESTER STATE HOSPITAL. Belmont Street, opp. Shrewsbury Street. Branch, Summer Street, between East Central and Asylum. Trustees-William J. Delehanty, Edward F. Fletcher, Howard W. Cowee, John H. Perman, William J. Thayer, Mrs. Frank Dresser, Mrs. Anna C. Tatman. Treasurer and Clerk-Jessie M. D. Hamilton. Superintendent-William A. Bryan. Assistant Superintendent- Frances H. Sleeper. Assistant Physicians-Michael J. O'Meara, S. Spafford Ackerly, Clifton T. Perkins, Arthur T. Whitney, Nathan Baratt, Morris Yushis, Lyman Orton, Jacob Goldwyn. Dentist-Alfred J. Normandin. Supt. of Nurses-Ann F. McElholm. Matron-Lillian G. Carr. Steward-Herbert W. Smith. Farmer-Wallace F. Garrett. Engineer-William P. Boyd. The Worcester State Hospital, a State institution, was opened January 18, 1833. At present there are 2,223 inmates.

GRAFTON STATE HOSPITAL. North Grafton, Mass. Trustees-Frank B. Hall, Chairman; Margaret A. Cashman, Sec.; Francis Prescott, Ernest L. Anderson, Flora M. Cangiano, Enos H. Bigelow, Winslow P. Burhoe. Superintendent-Harlan L. Paine. Asst. Supt.-Hiram L. Horsman; Assistant Physicians-James L. McAuslan, Mary Johnson, Wilbur H. Smith, John P. Powers, John P. Tierney, Anna C. Wellington. The Grafton State Hospital, formerly known as the Worcester State Asylum, was established in accordance with an Act of the Legislature in 1877, and was opened October 23, 1877. The present number of patients is 1,552.

Worcester Musicians' Association-LOCAL 143, A. F. of M.-Pres., Leo X. Fontaine; Vice-Pres., R. S. Twiss; Sec., F. R. Profit; Treas., William A. Harbour; Business Agent, Walter Hazelhurst. Meets second Sunday of each month 12 m. at 34 Front street. Officers elected in December.

Worcester Natural History Society-Org. 1829. 12 State street corner Harvard. This society was united with the Young Men's Library Association in 1856, under the name of the Lyceum and Library Association. In 1866 it was incorporated under the name of the Worcester Lyceum and Natural History Association. In 1884, by an act of the Legislature, the name was changed to that of the Worcester Natural History Society. Directors, Pres., Lemuel F. Woodward; Sec., Joseph H. Perry; Treas., Wendell P. Parker, Charles M. Thayer, Mrs. Frank E. Lowe; Acting Custodian, Mrs. John W. Kemp. The museum of the society at 12 State street, is open free to all daily, except Sunday and holidays from 9 a.m. to 12 p.m. and 2 to 5 p.m. Number of active voting members of the society is limited to fifty.

Worcester Oratorio Society-Org. 1897. Re-org. 1905. Pres., Albert H. Inman; Vice-Pres., J. Harvey Curtis; Sec., Charles E. Sargent; Treas., William H. Beaumont Directors, Charles Baker, J. Edward Bouvier, J. Vernon Butler, W. Gray Harris, F. P. Knowles, Samuel G. Rea, Chas. E. Sargent, Wm. H. Sawyer, Jr., H. R. Sinclair, W. Virgil Spaulding, Matthew P. Whittall; Conductors, J. Vernon Butler. Meets Monday evenings from October to January, in Union church, and gives annual series of concerts. Meeting for election of officers subject to call.
